title: Provincial Concentrator Account Deposits (BC)
body_text:
Provincial Concentrator Account Deposits (BC)
1	Introduction
1.1	Overview of Provincial Concentrator Account Deposits
The Province of BC, a BC Services office, or a business on behalf of the Province of BC can request to open a Province of BC Concentrator holding account on behalf of the Province of BC with a local financial institution.
Your financial institution uses the Province of BC Concentrator holding account to accept deposits for the Province of BC. The account acts as a pass-through account for settlement with Central 1 and the Province of BC via the Central 1 Provincial Concentrator Account. The Central 1 Provincial Concentrator Accounts is in Canadian funds. You must convert cash and/or cheques received in US dollars to Canadian dollars using the daily posted exchange rate prior to deposit.
On the day deposits are made, your financial institution credits the appropriate Provincial Concentrator holding account, submits a Province of BC Deposit Slip in ServiceNow, and processes cheques included in the deposit in Branch Capture. Deposits are reported in Canadian dollars. Central 1 generates a pre-authorized debit (PAD) against each account (the sweep) and credits the Province of BC. Each Provincial Concentrator holding account should be at a zero balance once the sweep is processed.
Provincial Concentrator holding accounts are deposit-taking accounts only. Central 1 is the only organization permitted to debit these accounts to aggregate funds for credit to the Province of BC. The accounts must never be in an overdraft position and canât have transaction fees or overdraft fees posted to them. However, financial institutions can obtain reimbursement for regular account service charges from the Province of BC. See Section 3.4, Reimbursement for Provincial Concentrator Holding Account Service Charges and Other Fees.

You identify an error in a Province of BC Deposit Slip
To confirm the names of Province of BC employees authorized to inquire about the Provincial Concentrator Accounts
